summary:
The chi-square test for independence should be used to compare the proportion of extubation failures between the traditional and bubble CPAP methods due to their suitability with dichotomous data across independent groups. This approach effectively evaluates whether any observed differences in failure rates could have occurred by chance alone. 
The chi-square test assesses these discrete event counts making it ideal when dealing directly with such binomially-distributed outcome measures as successful/extubated status within each intervention arm. Thus, this non-parametric technique provides an accurate assessment without assuming normal distribution requirements typical of parametric tests like Student’s T-Test applied incorrectly herein based upon provided context about distinct patient cohorts under differing ventilatory strategies during post-extubation care scenarios.

The chi-square test assesses these discrete event counts making it ideal when dealing directly with such binomially-distributed outcome measures as successful/extubated status within each intervention arm.
